title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE SIMATA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ PARASAN ANNEX IN BARANGAY PARASAN, MUNICIPALITY OF MOLAVE, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR FROM THE SIMATA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS PARASAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
108 OG No. 50, 6326 (December 10, 2012 )

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10226, October 18, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE SIMATA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ PARASAN ANNEX IN BARANGAY PARASAN, MUNICIPALITY OF MOLAVE, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR FROM THE SIMATA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS PARASAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

ГўВЂВ“

The Simata National High School ГўВЂВ“ Parasan Annex in Barangay Parasan,

Municipality of Molave, Province of Zamboanga del Sur is hereby

separated from the Simata National High School and converted into an

independent national high school to be known as Parasan National High

School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

ГўВЂВ“ All

personnel, records, assets and liabilities of the Simata National High

School ГўВЂВ“ Parasan Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the

Parasan National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

ГўВЂВ“ The Secretary of Education

shall immediately include in the DepartmentГўВЂВ™s program the

operationalization of the Parasan National High School, the initial

funding of which shall be charged against the current yearГўВЂВ™s

appropriations of the Simata National High School ГўВЂВ“ Parasan Annex.

Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the

school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

ГўВЂВ“ The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

Sec. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ“ This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was

finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on August

22, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

OCT 18 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
